steps.title: Use the graph, not invented equations
steps.content: The problem does not give formulas for the line or the absolute value graph. The solution to a system is the point where the two graphs intersect, so read the marked black point directly from the grid.

steps.title: Read the coordinates
steps.content: The black point is halfway between $-4$ and $-3$ on the $x$-axis, so $x=-3.5=-\frac{7}{2}$. It is also halfway between $4$ and $5$ on the $y$-axis, so $y=4.5=\frac{9}{2}$. Therefore the intersection is $$\left(-\frac{7}{2},\frac{9}{2}\right).$$

steps.title: Check the answer choices visually
steps.content:
• A: $(0,8)$ is on the $y$-axis, not at the marked intersection.
  • B: $\left(\frac{7}{2},\frac{9}{2}\right)$ has a positive $x$-coordinate, but the marked point is left of the $y$-axis.
  • C: $\left(-\frac{7}{2},\frac{9}{2}\right)$ matches the marked point.
  • D: $(-3,4)$ is the vertex of the V-shaped graph, not the intersection.
C is correct.
